beats.narration: Film and digital detectors record an image to study later. Fluoroscopy is different — it turns X rays into light you watch live.

payload.title: WATCH IT LIVE
payload.sub: X-rays become visible light the instant they arrive.

beats.narration: A fluorescent screen glows where radiation gets through. An image intensifier multiplies that faint light thousands of times to a camera and monitor.

payload.heading: From faint glow to bright monitor

beats.narration: The live view earns its keep three ways: scan parts fast, sort good from bad, and watch mechanisms move under load.
The trade is sharpness. A thick, fast screen spreads light sideways — screen unsharpness. Higher conversion efficiency means a brighter image at lower dose.
So fluoroscopy trades fine detail for live speed. And one rule never bends — view through glass, mirrors, or a monitor. Never the beam.

payload.sub: View through leaded glass, mirrors, or a monitor — always.
